title: quercus aegilops subsp. persica
intro: Quercus aegilops subsp. persica, also known as the Persian oak, is a subspecies of the Quercus aegilops tree, native to the mountainous regions of the Caucasus and Iran. This tree has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to its high tannin content. The health benefits of Quercus aegilops subsp. persica include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and protect against infections. Traditionally, the bark and leaves of the plant are used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in the production of medicinal tinctures and ointments.
